title: Purification of genomic DNA
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"77uqu","text":"This protocol details the purification of genomic DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: We used COBAS DNA Sample Preparation Kit, commercially available, from Roche (Catalog No. 05985536190) and some modifications to the manufacture instructions were made. The DNA pellet preserved at -20 °C should be equilibrated first to Room temperature prior to start the DNA purification protocol.
Add 200 µL DNA PBB to each tube, mix by pipetting and incubate the tubes at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .
Flash spin tubes at Room temperature to remove drops from inside of lid.
Allow the tubes to cool to 15 °C to 30 °C .
Place tubes in the 90 °C dry heat block and incubate for 01:00:00 .
Place tubes in 56 °C incubator with agitation/rotation for 01:00:00 .

If required for the test, place a negative control tube adding 180 µL DNA TLB and 70 µL of reconstituted Proteinase K. Vortex for 00:00:10 . The negative control should be processed following the same procedure as the samples.
Add 70 µL of reconstituted Proteinase K to the same 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube and vortex for 00:00:10 .
Resuspend the DNA pellet in 180 µL DNA TLB and mix for 00:00:10 by vortexing.
Centrifuge FT/CT units at 8000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute . Discard the flow-through. Place the FT into a new CT.

Add 500 µL working WB II to each FT. Prepare working WB II by adding 50 mL of absolute ethanol to the bottle of WB II. Mix by inverting the bottle 5 to 10 times. Label de bottle. Store working WB I at 15 to 30 for up to 90 days or until the expiration date, whichever comes first.
Centrifuge FT/CT units at 8000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute . Discard the flow-through. Place the FT back into the same CT.

Add 500 µL working WB I to each FT. Prepare working WB I by adding 15 mL of absolute ethanol to the bottle of WB I. Mix by inverting the bottle 5 to 10 times. Label de bottle. Store working WB I at 15 to 30 for up to 90 days or until the expiration date, whichever comes first.
Place the FT in a new CT and discard the old CT with the flow-through.
Centrifuge the FT/CT units at 8000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute .
Transfer the lysate into a Filter Tube (FT)/Collection Tube (CT) unit.
Add 100 µL isopropanol to each tube and mix lysate by pipetting.
Preserve the elution tube containing the DNA stock at -20 °C until quantification.
Centrifuge the FT with elution tube at 8000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute to collect eluate into the elution tube. Eluted DNA is now in the flow-through, do not discard.
Incubate the FT with elution tube 15 °C to 30 °C for 00:05:00 .
Add 100 µL DNA EB directly to the FT membrane.
Place each FT into an elution tube (1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube).
Centrifuge FT/CT units at 14000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute to dry the filter membranes. Discard the CT with the flow-through.

We used Quant-iT PicoGreen dsDNA Reagent and Kit, commercially available, from Invitrogen (Catalog No. P11496) and some modifications to the manufacture instructions were made. Prepare the DNA standard curve from DNA standard original stock 100 μg/ml storage at 4 °C .
Prepare 8 tubes and label A to H for the standards.

Perform dilution of standard as follows: Add 5 µL of Standard to tube A, mix by pipetting, and take 250 µL to transfer this volume to tube B. Change tips between dilutions. The dilutions must be mixed vigorously before transferring to the next tube for serial dilution.
See below appropriate volume of TE for each standard dilutions:
Prepare 20 mL of 1x working solution of TE from 20x TE. Use the nuclease free water for the dilution.
Final concentrations of the standards is as follows:
Tube H will only have TE. This is the blank control.
Mix very well and add 100 µL per well in the PicoGreen assay.
For DNA samples to be quantified perform a 1:50 dilution ratio with 10 µL DNA sample plus 490 µL 1x TE.
Draw a plate schematic making duplicates of standard and triplicate of sample to be quantified.
Using a multi-channel pipet, add 100 µL of picogreen solution to each well in a 96 well plate and cover with aluminum foil.
Picogreen comes in a 100 µL stock volume. Prepare a working solution with 1:200 ratio. Add 50 µL of picogreen solution into 9.95 mL of 1x TE.

Return the DNA samples to -20 °C after quantification. If the total preliminary DNA yield for a sample at this point is <350 ng, then process additional slides. The yields can be combined before the final DNA precipitation step. The goal is to achieve a minimal of 300 ng total DNA in the end of the process.
After read the plate, plot standard curve, and interpolate the samples to obtain protein measurement.

Using Cytation 3 imaging reader and Gen5 Image 2.07 software, read the plate using a fluorescence at 480 excitation and 520 emission. Read speed set to “normal” with 100 msec delay between reads. Set 2 gains: one automatic and second read set to 100 gain. With read height of 7mm.

Thaw DNA sample on water if previously frozen. If using two samples from the same patient because of insufficient yield, then pool the two samples before starting the ethanol precipitation procedure and make a note that volumes below need to be scaled up 2-fold or the doubled sample volume.
Centrifuge the mixture at 14000 x g, Room temperature, 15 minutes centrifuge to keep salt dissolved.
Wash the DNA pellet by adding Room temperature 70% ethanol made from nuclease free water. Close cap and flick tube slightly to expose bottom of pellet to ethanol wash.
Centrifuge the mixture at 14000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes centrifuge.
Incubate the mixture at -20 °C for 01:00:00 .
Mix gently but thoroughly, inverting 20 times.

Add 2.5 volume of ice cold ( -20 °C ) 100% ethanol (not isopropyl) to the mixture of DNA solution/sodium acetate/linear-acrylamide. [2.5 vol. x (vol. DNA solution + vol. sodium acetate + vol. linear-acrylamide)].
Add 2 µL linear-acrylamide (stock 5 mg/ml ) to DNA/sodium acetate mixture.
Add 1/10 DNA sample volume of 3 M sodium acetate to DNA solution (e.g., 9 µL NaAC per 90 µL remaining DNA sample after quantification).
Leave the tube upside-down opened for 00:05:00 or as needed to facilitate drying. Be careful with the position of the pellet on the tube. Pellets can become nearly invisible after drying.

Dissolve DNA in 10 millimolar (mM) nuclease free Tris, pH 8.0. When deciding on final volume to solubilize DNA, calculate what would be expected to produce 100 ng/µL based on total yield from first PicoGreen. If DNA is already between 50 ng-100 ng/µL (result from the first PicoGreen) do not dilute the sample. Otherwise, if the stock DNA is above 100 ng/µL, then proceed with the dilute down to 100ng/µL with 10 mM nuclease free Tris pH, 8.0.

We used Quant-iT PicoGreen dsDNA Reagent and Kit, commercially available, from Invitrogen (Catalog No. P11496) and some modifications to the manufacture instructions were made. Perform the same procedure than in First PicoGreen.
For DNA samples to be quantified make a 1:500 dilution ratio with 1 µL DNA sample plus 490 µL 1x TE.
Mix very well and add 100 µL per well in the PicoGreen assay. Sample DNA yields before ethanol precipitation
Add 495 µL of TE to tube A.
Add 250 µL of TE to tubes B, D and E.
Add 400 µL of TE to tube C.
Add 450 µL of TE to tubes F to H.
Take 100 µL from tube B and transfer the volume to tube C.
Take 250 µL from tube C and transfer the volume to tube D.
Take 250 µL from tube D and transfer the volume to tube E.
Take 50 µL from tube E and transfer the volume to tube F.
Take 50 µL from tube F and transfer the volume to tube G.
creator: Vlad Sandulache
